title: Irenea myriantha
uses: Irenea myriantha is known for its traditional medicinal uses, primarily in Africa. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. Its extracts are believed to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its medicinal applications. The leaves and roots of the plant are used in traditional medicine, often in combination with other herbs. Further research is needed to fully understand the potential health benefits and limitations of using Irenea myriantha as a medicinal plant.

The classification of Irenea myriantha places it within the order Gentianales and the class Magnoliopsida. The family Rubiaceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants, often referred to as the coffee or madder family. Irenea myriantha is a specific species within this family, with its own unique characteristics and growth habits.
